hardware: M2 MacBook Pro, 16GB RAM, Apple NVMe SSD
note: Estimated values — run `cargo bench` to measure on your hardware

benchmarks.note: BTreeMap insert; pure in-memory, no I/O. Limited by tree rebalancing at high cardinality.
Includes bit-packing overhead. Chunk flush (every 128 points) involves a JSON write to disk.
8 bytes raw vs ~0.7 bytes/point avg. Regular 1s timestamps compress to ~1 bit each (DoD=0).
v0: 16 bytes/point × 1M. v1: ~1.4 bytes/point avg with Gorilla on regular-interval data.
Decompress 3600 points (28 chunks). Bottleneck is bit-unpacking, not disk I/O.
